_meta.description: UAV component database for the Drone Designer MVP. Entries span small racing quads (~250mm) to large industrial UAVs (~1200mm+). All specs pulled from manufacturer datasheets. Prices are approximate USD retail as of 2024.

motors.name: F40 Pro IV
motors.notes: High-performance race motor. 2400KV variant optimal for 4S with 5-inch tri-blade props.

motors.name: Antigravity MN3110 KV780
motors.notes: Antigravity series — optimised for efficiency and smooth footage. Good for 4S/6S on 9-10 inch props.

motors.name: U8 Lite KV100
motors.notes: Premium industrial motor. Designed for long-endurance heavy-lift with 22-24 inch props on 12S.

motors.name: SunnySky X2212 KV980
motors.notes: Popular budget-friendly workhorse. Excellent value for entry-level builds. Pairs well with 9x4.5 props on 3S.

motors.name: MN4014 KV400
motors.notes: Strong mid-range industrial-class motor. Often used in survey drones with 15-inch CF props.

motors.name: KDE2315XF-885
motors.notes: MIL-SPEC grade motor built for reliability in harsh conditions. Popular in search-and-rescue and inspection UAVs.

motors.name: V-Spec 2807 KV1300
motors.notes: Popular 6S long-range racing/freestyle motor. Good balance of thrust and efficiency for 7-inch builds.

motors.name: Antigravity MN8014 KV120
motors.notes: Large-diameter low-KV motor for heavy-lift applications. Provides 7800 gf max thrust at 14.8V with 20-inch props, clearing the 7347 gf/motor requirement.

motors.name: Imaginary 2306 2400KV
motors.notes: Imaginary entry satisfying: 5.1x4.6 prop (designatedPropSizeInMin 4.5 <= 5.1 <= designatedPropSizeInMax 5.5), maxThrustG 520 >= 385 gf, voltageMinV 11.1 <= 14.8 <= voltageMaxV 16.8, motorMaxRpm 2400x14.8=35520 >= propHoverRpm 24500 x 1.4=34300.

motors.name: MN4010 KV430
motors.notes: Legacy low-KV survey/endurance option for 9-11 inch props. Retained for broad database coverage, but no longer used as the calibrated guaranteed-passing pair for prop_008 under the current ComputePropellerAero formula.

motors.name: U10 Lite KV830 Survey
motors.notes: Calibrated guaranteed-passing pair with prop_008 under the current ComputePropellerAero formula. At 1500 gf/motor on 4S at sea level, prop_008 requires about 8770 RPM and kvRequired about 830 after headroom. Shaft 6.0 mm fits the 6.35 mm bore with 0.35 mm clearance.

escs.name: XRotor Micro 45A 4-in-1
escs.notes: Stacks with Betaflight F7 or similar. Ideal for 4S/6S racing builds. Telemetry feeds back to FC.

escs.name: XRotor Pro 80A
escs.notes: Single ESC. Suitable for heavier motors in 6S photography/proximity builds.

escs.name: Flame 100A
escs.notes: Designed to pair with T-Motor U8/U10 series. Supports 12S — essential for high-voltage industrial builds.

escs.name: Platinum Pro 30A V4
escs.notes: Includes linear BEC. Good for builds without a separate UBEC. Compatible with SunnySky X2212.

escs.name: Tekko32 F4 Metal 65A 4-in-1
escs.notes: Metal housing for better heat dissipation under sustained load. Supports DSHOT1200 for lowest latency.

escs.name: Alpha 60A HV
escs.notes: High voltage rating up to 8S. Supports T-Motor ESC telemetry protocol.

escs.name: Typhoon HV 160A
escs.notes: Used in cinematography heavy-lift platforms. CAN bus interface available for integration with Pixhawk ecosystem.

pdbs.name: HV Power Distribution Board 300A
pdbs.notes: High-current PDB rated 300A continuous, clearing the 212A requirement for 4 motors at 14.8V. 8 ESC pads supports up to octocopter configurations.

propellers.name: HQProp 5.1x4.6x3-V1S
propellers.notes: Tri-blade for balanced thrust and efficiency. V1S is the newest version with improved tip geometry.

propellers.name: T-Style 9x4.5 Inch
propellers.notes: Efficient low-noise design. Pairs well with Antigravity MN3110 motor series.

propellers.name: P16x5.4 CF Folding
propellers.notes: Folding design for easy transport. Carbon fiber construction keeps mass low at this size. Requires folding prop hub adapter.

propellers.name: APC 10x4.7 SF
propellers.notes: Slow Fly (SF) geometry optimised for efficiency at lower speeds. Popular choice for ArduPilot-based survey platforms.

propellers.name: Tarot 1555 Folding CF
propellers.notes: Folding prop commonly used in Tarot 680/810 hex frames. Robust folding hub mechanism.

propellers.name: HQProp 3x3x3 Micro
propellers.notes: For 1106/1306 motor class micro quads. Polycarbonate is durable for indoor flying.

propellers.name: T18x6.1 Carbon Fiber
propellers.notes: Designed to pair with T-Motor U8 series. Fixed-pitch version for direct bolt-on to motor shaft.

propellers.name: T-Style 10x4.7 CF Survey
propellers.notes: Ct/Cp estimated from APC 10x4.7 SF reference data (similar geometry, CF vs glass-filled nylon). Design-point metadata recalibrated for the current ComputePropellerAero formula: Ct=0.135 implies about 8770 RPM at 1500 gf sea level, and about 2800 gf at the 12000 RPM prop limit. Guaranteed-passing current-formula pair: prop_008 with mot_011 on 4S.

flightControllers.name: Kakute H7 V1.3
flightControllers.notes: H7 processor for high-frequency gyro sampling. Excellent choice for INAV long-range builds due to extra UARTs.

flightControllers.name: Pixhawk 4 Mini
flightControllers.recommendedUseCase: Photography quad, survey UAV, small fixed-wing
flightControllers.notes: Compact Pixhawk 4 variant. Dual IMU for redundancy. Supports full ArduPilot/PX4 feature set including LIDAR, optical flow.

flightControllers.name: Cube Orange+
flightControllers.notes: Triple-redundant IMUs, dual barometers. Industry standard for safety-critical UAV applications. Requires separate carrier board (Mini Carrier, Standard Carrier, etc.).

flightControllers.name: Pixhawk 6C
flightControllers.recommendedUseCase: Survey UAV, long-range fixed-wing, professional multi-rotor
flightControllers.notes: Latest generation Pixhawk with USB-C and dual CAN. H7 core with 16 PWM outputs supports large multi-rotor configurations.

flightControllers.name: Matek H743-WLITE
flightControllers.recommendedUseCase: DIY survey UAV, long-range FPV wing, VTOL
flightControllers.notes: Very popular with the DIY community. Low cost, H7 power, 7 UARTs. Great ArduPlane base for fixed-wing FPV.

flightControllers.name: CUAV V5+ Autopilot
flightControllers.notes: Designed for demanding professional applications. Includes vibration isolation for IMUs. Good alternative to Pixhawk 4 at similar price point.

batteries.name: R-Line Version 5.0 1550mAh 4S
batteries.notes: Flagship racing battery. High discharge, low internal resistance. Not suitable for high-efficiency long-endurance builds due to mass/capacity ratio.

batteries.name: Plus 10000mAh 6S
batteries.notes: Good endurance battery. AS150 connector required at this current rating. Monitor internal resistance over charge cycles.

batteries.name: Plus Smart 22000mAh 6S
batteries.notes: Smart battery with integrated cell-level telemetry via CAN bus. Self-discharge protection. Designed for industrial platforms.

batteries.name: Gens Ace 5200mAh 4S 60C
batteries.notes: Solid all-round battery. XT90 connector for high current draw. Suitable for 4S builds needing decent flight time and punch.

batteries.name: Tattu 16000mAh 6S 15C
batteries.notes: Long endurance optimised. Lower C rating means less internal heat. Designed for sustained moderate-current draw typical in survey missions.

batteries.name: Gens Ace 1300mAh 4S 45C
batteries.notes: Budget option for racing. Adequate performance; not competitive with Tattu R-Line at high discharge but much lower cost.

batteries.name: Tattu 30000mAh 12S 15C
batteries.notes: 12S configuration for highest voltage efficiency with U8/U10 motors. Requires proper BMS or balance charger supporting 12S.

batteries.name: Endurance 14000mAh 4S
batteries.notes: High-capacity 4S pack for extended flight missions. Meets 4S >= 13950mAh requirement at 920g (under 951g limit). 10C continuous rating satisfies 6.8C minimum.

batteries.name: Industrial 90000mAh 4S
batteries.recommendedUseCase: Heavy-lift industrial UAV, long-endurance cargo or survey platform
batteries.notes: Large-format industrial LiPo meeting >= 88500mAh at 4S. 3C continuous clears the 2.1C minimum. 16800g is within the 19498g mass limit.

batteries.name: Endurance 10000mAh 4S 15C
batteries.notes: Imaginary entry satisfying: 4S, 10000 mAh >= 9861 mAh, 15C >= 14.3C, 590 g <= 610 g.

gpsModules.name: M9N GPS Module
gpsModules.notes: Compact and affordable. Excellent multi-constellation tracking. Pairs well with Pixhawk 4 Mini and similar.

gpsModules.name: Here3 CAN GPS
gpsModules.notes: CAN bus interface provides noise-immune communication ideal for industrial platforms. DroneCAN protocol. Requires autopilot with CAN support (Cube Orange+, Pixhawk 6C).

gpsModules.name: Here+ RTK GPS v2
gpsModules.notes: RTK capable — 2.5cm accuracy in RTK fixed mode. Requires USB base station unit sold separately. Essential for GCP-free photogrammetry workflows.

gpsModules.name: Emlid Reach M2
gpsModules.notes: Multi-band (L1/L2) RTK. Centimetre-level accuracy in RTK fixed mode. Connects to NTRIP for network RTK corrections. Requires separate GNSS antenna.

gpsModules.name: Pixhawk M10 GPS
gpsModules.notes: Newest generation Holybro GPS. NavIC constellation support improves coverage in South Asia. Good upgrade to M8N-based modules.

gpsModules.name: BN-220 GPS/GLONASS
gpsModules.notes: Ultra-compact and lightweight. No compass — requires external magnetometer. Popular for INAV/Betaflight builds on weight-constrained platforms.

telemetryRadios.name: SiK Telemetry Radio V3 915MHz
telemetryRadios.notes: Open-source SiK firmware. Pairs out of the box with Mission Planner and QGroundControl. 915 MHz for North America (433 MHz variant available for EU).

telemetryRadios.name: RFD900x
telemetryRadios.notes: Industry-standard long-range radio. Up to 40km LoS. AES-128 encryption option. Multi-point networking capability. Widely used in commercial ArduPilot deployments.

telemetryRadios.name: Herelink HD Video Transmission Air Unit
telemetryRadios.recommendedUseCase: Cinematic UAV, inspection with live HD video
telemetryRadios.notes: Combines HD video + RC control + MAVLink telemetry in single link. Ground unit is Android-based controller with 5.5-inch screen. Supports HDMI and AV input on air unit.

telemetryRadios.name: TBS Crossfire Micro TX V2
telemetryRadios.notes: RC link primarily — not a full telemetry system like RFD900x. Very low latency (5ms). CRSF protocol integrates with Betaflight/INAV for telemetry passthrough. Requires CRSF-compatible TX module on controller side.

telemetryRadios.name: Microhard FHSS 900MHz pDDL1800
telemetryRadios.notes: Industrial grade datalink. Designed for mission-critical BVLOS applications. Supports simultaneous voice/data/video in some configurations.

telemetryRadios.name: ESP8266 WiFi Telemetry
telemetryRadios.recommendedUseCase: Indoor testing, bench work, <100m development use
telemetryRadios.notes: Short-range only. Useful for local bench testing with Mission Planner via WiFi. Not suitable for field operations. Flash with ESP-MAVLink firmware.

telemetryRadios.name: Herelink Air Unit
telemetryRadios.notes: Integrated HD video + MAVLink telemetry system. 10km typical range clears the 6.3km requirement (5.0km x 1.25 margin). Includes ground station controller unit.

cameras.name: GoPro HERO 12 Black
cameras.recommendedUseCase: FPV cinematic footage, inspection, low-cost aerial photo
cameras.notes: Widely used for aerial video on small/mid quads. Requires gimbal for stabilised footage. GP-2 processor handles 5.3K with HyperSmooth 6.0.

cameras.name: Sony ZV-E10 II
cameras.notes: High-quality interchangeable lens camera. Requires gimbal and weatherproofing housing. Gimbals like Gremsy H3 or Zenmuse X7 can mount this via custom plate.

cameras.name: FLIR Vue TZ20-R
cameras.recommendedUseCase: Search and rescue, infrastructure inspection, precision agriculture
cameras.notes: Dual thermal + EO sensor. Radiometric TIFF captures absolute temperature data per pixel. Essential for thermographic inspection workflows.

cameras.name: MicaSense RedEdge-P
cameras.recommendedUseCase: Precision agriculture NDVI mapping, crop health analysis
cameras.notes: Industry gold standard for agricultural UAV multispectral imaging. Requires MicaSense DLS-2 downwelling light sensor for accurate reflectance calibration. Includes calibration reflectance panel.

cameras.name: RunCam Phoenix 2 Joshua Edition
cameras.notes: Analog FPV camera optimised for low latency. Starlight sensor for night flying. Pairs with any analog VTX. No OSD — use FC-side OSD overlay.

cameras.name: Foxeer Apollo Mini
cameras.notes: Compact micro form factor. Good low-light performance. Popular for indoor racing and toothpick builds.

cameras.name: DJI O3 Air Unit
outputInterfaces: DJI O3 RF link to goggles
cameras.notes: Digital FPV with up to 10km range to DJI Goggles 3. 1080p/100fps mode for slow-motion. OSD over DJI link. Requires DJI-compatible controller/goggles. Cannot mix with analog VTX.

cameras.name: Sony ILX-LR1
cameras.recommendedUseCase: Professional survey mapping, LiDAR integration platform
cameras.notes: Purpose-built payload camera with no display/viewfinder, optimised for weight. Ethernet trigger and control for automated survey workflows. Requires Sony E-mount lens — typically 24mm f/2.8 for mapping work.

cameras.name: Zenmuse P1
cameras.notes: 45MP full-frame sensor. 8192x5460 = ~44.7 MP. Industry standard for high-accuracy photogrammetry. Integrates with DJI M300/M350 RTK.

cameras.name: Sony UMC-R10C
cameras.recommendedUseCase: Mapping, inspection, survey on mid-size UAV
cameras.notes: 20MP APS-C sensor. 5456x3632 = ~19.8 MP. Lightweight for its class. Good GSD at moderate altitude for corridor and area surveys.

cameras.name: MicaSense RedEdge-P
cameras.notes: 5-band multispectral + panchromatic. 4608x3456 = ~15.9 MP per band. Industry standard for crop health and agricultural survey missions.

receivers.name: R-XSR
receivers.notes: Ultra-lightweight full-range receiver. Redundant antennas. S.Port telemetry support.

receivers.name: TBS Crossfire Micro RX V2
receivers.notes: 900MHz link with exceptional penetration and range. CRSF protocol gives low-latency bidirectional link to ArduPilot and Betaflight FCs.

receivers.name: EP2 ELRS Receiver
receivers.notes: Open-source ExpressLRS protocol. Excellent latency and range at low cost. 2.4GHz with up to 10km in ideal conditions.

receivers.name: R9 Mini
receivers.notes: 900MHz long-range receiver in a compact form factor. Pairs with R9M module. Good middle ground between Crossfire and standard 2.4GHz receivers.
